name: NvidiaNIM_alphafold2
description: Predict protein 3D structure from amino acid sequence using DeepMind's AlphaFold2 via NVIDIA NIM. Returns PDB format structure. Requires NVIDIA_API_KEY. Input: sequence (amino acids), algorithm (jackhmmer/mmseqs2), databases (uniref90, mgnify, small_bfd). Long-running async operation.

sequence.description: Amino acid sequence to predict structure for (single letter codes)

algorithm.description: MSA search algorithm. mmseqs2 is faster, jackhmmer is more sensitive

databases.description: Sequence databases for MSA search: uniref90, mgnify, small_bfd

return_schema.description: AlphaFold2 structure prediction result with PDB coordinates, confidence scores, and model metrics

name: NvidiaNIM_alphafold2_multimer
description: Predict multi-chain protein complex structure using AlphaFold2-Multimer via NVIDIA NIM. Input: sequences (array of 1-6 protein sequences for complex). Returns predicted complex structure. Long-running async operation.

sequences.description: Array of 1-6 protein sequences (amino acid single letter codes) to predict as a complex

databases.description: Sequence databases for MSA: uniref90, small_bfd

relax_prediction.description: Whether to relax the predicted structure

return_schema.description: AlphaFold2-Multimer complex structure prediction with multi-chain PDB coordinates and confidence scores

name: NvidiaNIM_esmfold
description: Fast alignment-free protein structure prediction using ESMFold via NVIDIA NIM. No MSA required - predicts directly from sequence. Input: sequence (max 1024 amino acids). Returns PDB structure. Fast synchronous response.

sequence.description: Amino acid sequence (max 1024 chars, valid: ARNDCQEGHILKMFPSTWYVXBOU)

return_schema.description: PDB format structure text (returned via 'structure' field, not 'data'). Contains ATOM records with coordinates and pLDDT confidence in B-factor column.

name: NvidiaNIM_openfold2
description: Predict protein structure from sequence and MSA using OpenFold2 via NVIDIA NIM. PyTorch reimplementation of AlphaFold2 with improved speed. Input: sequence plus pre-computed MSA alignments in a3m format. Long-running async operation.

alignments.description: MSA alignments by database. Format: {db_name: {a3m: {alignment: str, format: 'a3m'}}}

selected_models.description: Model indices to use for prediction (1-5)

return_schema.description: OpenFold2 structure prediction with PDB coordinates and model quality metrics

name: NvidiaNIM_openfold3
description: Predict biomolecular complex structure (proteins, DNA, RNA, ligands) using OpenFold3 via NVIDIA NIM. AlphaFold3-style prediction for complete biological assemblies. Input: molecules (proteins with MSA, DNA, RNA, ligands). Long-running async operation.

items.description: Molecule spec: type (protein/dna/rna/ligand), sequence, msa, etc.

inputs.description: List of input specifications for structure prediction

return_schema.description: OpenFold3 biomolecular complex structure prediction (proteins, DNA, RNA, ligands) with coordinates and confidence metrics

name: NvidiaNIM_boltz2
description: Predict multi-molecular complex structure (proteins, DNA, RNA, ligands) using Boltz2 via NVIDIA NIM. Supports protein-protein, protein-DNA/RNA, protein-ligand complexes. Input: polymers (proteins/DNA/RNA), ligands (SMILES or CCD codes). Long-running async operation.

ligands.description: Ligands specified by CCD code OR SMILES (max 20)

return_schema.description: Boltz2 multi-molecular complex structure prediction (proteins, DNA, RNA, ligands) in mmCIF format with confidence scores

name: NvidiaNIM_proteinmpnn
description: Design protein sequences for a given backbone structure using ProteinMPNN via NVIDIA NIM (inverse folding). Input: PDB structure text. Output: designed sequences in Multi-FASTA format with log-probabilities. Fast synchronous response.

input_pdb.description: PDB format text of the backbone structure (ATOM records)

ca_only.description: Use only CA atoms for design

use_soluble_model.description: Use model trained on soluble proteins

sampling_temp.description: Sampling temperatures (0.1-0.3 recommended for high quality)

return_schema.description: ProteinMPNN inverse folding result with designed sequences in Multi-FASTA format and per-residue log-probabilities

name: NvidiaNIM_rfdiffusion
description: De novo protein design using RFdiffusion via NVIDIA NIM. Generate novel protein structures, binders, or scaffold motifs. Input: contigs DSL (e.g., 'A20-60/0 50-100'), input_pdb (required for scaffolding), hotspot_res for binding sites. Long-running async operation.

contigs.description: Contig specification DSL. Format: 'ChainStart-End/gap length'. Example: 'A20-60/0 50-100' keeps residues A20-60 and generates 50-100 new residues

input_pdb.description: PDB structure for scaffolding/binder design (ATOM records only). Required by the API.

return_schema.description: RFdiffusion de novo protein design result with generated PDB structure coordinates

name: NvidiaNIM_diffdock
description: Blind molecular docking using DiffDock via NVIDIA NIM. Predict ligand binding poses without specifying binding pocket. Input: protein (PDB content or asset ID), ligand (SDF content or asset ID). For large files, use is_staged=true with asset IDs. Returns docking poses with confidence scores.

protein.description: Protein structure: PDB content directly OR asset ID (if is_staged=true)

ligand.description: Ligand structure: SDF/MOL2 content directly OR asset ID (if is_staged=true)

num_poses.description: Number of docking poses to generate (1-40)

is_staged.description: If true, protein and ligand are asset IDs (uploaded via NVCF assets API). If false, they are raw file content.

return_schema.description: DiffDock blind molecular docking result with predicted ligand binding poses and confidence scores

name: NvidiaNIM_genmol
description: Generate molecules using GenMol via NVIDIA NIM. Input: SMILES/SAFE template with masked regions. Output: generated molecules with property scores. Use [*{min-max}] syntax for masked fragments. Fast synchronous response.

smiles.description: SAFE/SMILES template with masked fragments. Use [*{min-max}] for variable-length masks. Example: 'CC(=O)O[*{5-10}]'

return_schema.description: GenMol generated molecules with SMILES strings and property scores (QED or LogP)

name: NvidiaNIM_molmim
description: Controlled molecule generation using MolMIM via NVIDIA NIM. Generates molecules with optimized properties based on a reference molecule. Input: smi (SMILES string), num_molecules, algorithm (CMA-ES, GA, etc.). Output: generated molecules with property scores. Fast synchronous response.

smi.description: Reference SMILES string for molecule generation

algorithm.description: Optimization algorithm: CMA-ES (Covariance Matrix Adaptation Evolution Strategy) or GA (Genetic Algorithm)

return_schema.description: MolMIM controlled molecule generation result with optimized SMILES and property scores

name: NvidiaNIM_evo2
description: Generate DNA sequences using Evo2-40B via NVIDIA NIM. 40 billion parameter model trained on 9 trillion nucleotides. Input: DNA sequence (ACTG), num_tokens to generate. Output: generated sequence with optional per-nucleotide probabilities. Fast synchronous response.

sequence.description: Input DNA sequence (A, C, T, G characters only)

return_schema.description: Evo2 DNA sequence generation result with generated nucleotide sequence and optional per-nucleotide probabilities

name: NvidiaNIM_msa_search
description: GPU-accelerated multiple sequence alignment search using ColabFold/MMseqs2 via NVIDIA NIM. Input: protein sequence. Output: MSA alignments in a3m/fasta format. Use for structure prediction pipelines. Long-running async operation.

sequence.description: Protein sequence to search (1-4096 amino acids)

iterations.description: Number of search iterations for sensitivity

return_schema.description: MSA search result with multiple sequence alignments in a3m/fasta format from database searches

name: NvidiaNIM_esm2_650m
description: Generate protein sequence embeddings using ESM2-650M via NVIDIA NIM. 650 million parameter language model for protein sequences. Input: sequences (array of protein sequences, max 1024 AA each), format (npz/json). Output: embedding vectors in specified format. Returns binary npz file or JSON. Fast synchronous response.

sequences.description: Array of protein sequences (max 1024 amino acids each, valid: ARNDCQEGHILKMFPSTWYVXBOU)

format.description: Output format: npz (binary NumPy archive) or json

return_schema.description: Binary NPZ or JSON embedding data. For NPZ format: '<Binary data, N bytes>'. For JSON format: embedding vectors as nested arrays.

name: NvidiaNIM_maisi
description: Generate synthetic CT images with segmentation masks using MAISI via NVIDIA NIM. 3D Latent Diffusion Model for medical imaging data augmentation. Input: body_region, anatomy_list, controllable_anatomy_size, output_size. Returns ZIP file with NIfTI/NRRD images and labels. Long-running async operation.

num_output_samples.description: Number of CT images to generate

anatomy_list.description: Specific anatomical structures to include (e.g., liver, spleen, kidney)

items.description: [anatomy_name, size_ratio] pairs for controlling structure sizes

controllable_anatomy_size.description: Control anatomy sizes as [[name, ratio], ...]. Example: [['hepatic tumor', 0.3], ['liver', 0.5]]

output_size.description: Output volume size [x, y, z]. x,y: 256-512, z: 128-768

spacing.description: Voxel spacing [x, y, z] in mm

pre_signed_url.description: Optional pre-signed URL to upload results to

return_schema.description: ZIP file containing synthetic CT images (NIfTI/NRRD) and segmentation masks. Returned as '<ZIP file, N bytes>'.

name: NvidiaNIM_vista3d
description: 3D medical image segmentation using VISTA-3D via NVIDIA NIM. Segment organs and structures from CT scans. Input: image URL (NIfTI/NRRD), class prompts, optional point prompts for interactive refinement. Returns ZIP file with segmentation masks. Synchronous response.

image.description: URL to NIfTI or NRRD CT image file

points.description: Optional point prompts per class for interactive refinement. Format: {class_name: [[x,y,z], ...]}

prompts.description: Segmentation prompts: classes and optional point coordinates

return_schema.description: ZIP file containing 3D segmentation masks (NIfTI/NRRD) for requested anatomical structures. Returned as '<ZIP file, N bytes>'.
